response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced web and backend software developer with a broad full-stack background, but his profile has no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, hardware-software co-design, and deep-learning workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency application work using async I/O in DLang at Sociomantic, but that is a software-level concern, not hardware architecture. There is no evidence of GPU architecture knowledge, SIMD/MIMD experience, or any engagement with silicon-level or AI infrastructure work.
strengths: 15+ years of varied engineering experience demonstrates adaptability and technical breadth across multiple paradigms and languages
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, performance-sensitive design) shows some performance engineering awareness
Meets the minimum educational and experience threshold (BS + 8+ years) required by the role
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in AI/deep-learning workload analysis, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator optimization
No hardware-software co-design or silicon-level architecture experience; entire career is in application and web-layer software
